target.name: Impossible Metals
target.founder: Oliver Gunasekara (CEO, 30+ yrs tech, led ARM mobile growing mobile share 0%→95%), Jason Gillham (subsea-robotics, founded 2G Robotics/Voyis acq Jan 2020, 18 yrs industry), Renee Grogan (Director, sustainability/extractive resources)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Impossible Metals is a Seed-stage frontier-tech company in Climate & Energy, founded by Oliver Gunasekara (30+ years in technology), Jason Gillham (18 years in subsea robotics; founded 2G Robotics, acquired by Voyis in January 2020), and Renee Grogan (sustainability and extractive resources). The company has raised $15M to date. The Climate & Energy sector includes one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private peers, though comparable companies span diverse sub-segments and stages.

Given the nascent stage and sector heterogeneity, only the stage-progression method is applicable. Using total capital raised ($15M) as a value proxy and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to next stage yields $38M. A peer-median approach was not feasible; while nine companies operate in Climate & Energy, none met minimum thresholds (≥3 datapoints) for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier filtering. Applying a broad sector median would introduce substantial noise given the fragmented competitive landscape.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $32M to $43M, centered on $38M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression calculation. Key uncertainties include the sustainability of Seed-stage step-up multiples in climate tech (where capital efficiency and technical risk profiles vary widely) and the absence of true operational precedent among near-peer companies at this exact stage and sub-sector position.
